data.title: Seaport, Hemab price upsized IPOs eyeing $550M in proceeds
data.description:
Hemab Therapeutics (COAG), a drug developer targeting bleeding disorders, and neuropsychiatric drug developer Seaport Therapeutics (SPTX) have priced their upsized initial public offerings ahead of their trading debuts on Friday, seeking to raise more than $550M in total.

Cambridge, Massachusetts-based Hemab (COAG) priced its IPO offering of 16.75M shares of its common stock at $18.00 each, raising roughly $301.5M in gross proceeds. Underwriters will have a 30-day option to buy up to 2.5M additional shares.

The clinical-stage biotech previously sought to offer approximately 11.8M shares in the $16–$18 per share range.

Meanwhile, Seaport (SPTX), led by biotech veteran Daphne Zohar, priced its offering of nearly 14.2M shares at $18.00 each, expecting $254.9M in gross proceeds. 2.1M shares will be available additionally as part of a 30-day option to underwriters.

The Boston, Massachusetts-based firm previously sought to sell approximately 11.8M shares priced at $16 - $18 each. Both companies indicated a May 4 timeline to close their respective offerings.

data.title: Most and least shorted large-cap tech stocks in April
data.description:
Short interest across the tech sector shows a clear divide, with heavily shorted positions concentrated in smaller, high-beta names, while large-cap leaders continue to see minimal bearish bets.

Here’s a list of the 10 most shorted technology stocks (market cap above $2B) as of April:

  • SoundHound AI (SOUN): 37.24%
  • Ondas (ONDS): 33.58%
  • CleanSpark (CLSK): 33.00%
  • Applied Digital (APLD): 28.02%
  • MARA Holdings (MARA): 27.54%
  • Bitdeer Technologies Group (BTDR): 26.66%
  • Quantum Computing (QUBT): 24.35%
  • UiPath (PATH): 23.63%
  • IonQ (IONQ): 22.92%
  • Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS): 21.05%On the other end, the least shorted tech stocks (market cap above $2B) include:
  • Ubiquiti (UI) 0.63%
  • Apple (AAPL) 0.92%
  • Silicon Motion Technology (SIMO) 0.92%
  • EverCommerce (EVCM) 0.92%
  • Oracle (ORCL) 1.02%
  • Amphenol (APH) 1.12%
  • Microsoft (MSFT) 1.12%
  • Broadcom (AVGO) 1.15%
  • NVIDIA  (NVDA) 1.17%
  • Keysight Technologies (KEYS) 1.18%Popular technology ETFs include the Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK), Vanguard Information Technology ETF (VGT), iShares U.S. Technology ETF (IYW), VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH), iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX), and the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ), which offer broad and thematic exposure to large-cap technology and semiconductor stocks

data.title: Wall Street opens higher as May trading starts with oil falling
data.description:
The stock market opened higher as Wall Street looked to kick off the month of May on an optimistic note, with oil prices retreating and corporate earnings going strong. 

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.8%, the Dow (DJI) +0.6%, and the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +1.2%.

U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude futures (CL1:COM) gapped down 5% to $99.79 a barrel after Iran reportedly submitted its response via Pakistani mediators to the latest U.S. amendments to a draft deal to end the Middle East war. International benchmark Brent crude futures (CO1:COM) edged down 3.1% to above ~$106.93 a barrel.

Apple (AAPL) was in the spotlight, with its shares advancing 5.5% in morning trading, after the technology giant turned in better-than-expected results for fiscal Q2, as the iPhone 17 line continued to impress consumers.

Investors were also assessing fresh reads on U.S. manufacturing, with April S&P U.S. Manufacturing PMI rising slightly more than expected from a month earlier. The separate U.S. ISM Manufacturing PMI was unchanged in April and fell short of the consensus.

U.S. Treasury yields reversed lower across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.35%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) slipped 1 basis point to 3.86%. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) was down 2 basis points to 4.95%.

data.title: S&P Global mulls changing IPO rules for megacap companies
data.description:
S&P Global (SPGI) said on Thursday that its unit S&P Dow Jones Indices is considering rule changes for megacap companies seeking to IPO into its indexes.

S&P DJI defines megacaps as any company with a market capitalization equal to or greater than the 100th largest company in the S&P Total Market Index.

The proposed changes would apply to S&P 500 (SP500), S&P MidCap 400 (SP400), and S&P SmallCap 600 (SP600) indexes.

The unit has launched a consultation on proposals that include a modification to the seasoning period. The companies are required to trade on an eligible exchange for at least 12 months before being considered for addition to an index. The proposed changes include cutting the time to six months.

Additionally, the index provider is mulling waiving off the minimum 0.10 IWF eligibility requirement and Financial Viability Criteria requirement.

The Investible Weight Factor, or IWF, determines the percentage of a company's outstanding shares available for trading in the public market.

The Financial Viability Criteria ensures a company is financially healthy enough to be considered a representative of the market.

Furthermore, S&P DJI is considering changing the IWF-related rules for companies seeking an entry into the S&P Total Market Index (SPTMI), S&P Completion Index (SPCMI), and Dow Jones U.S. Total Stock Market Index (DWCF).

For companies seeking an entry into the S&P Composite 1500 Index (SP1500), the proposed rule change includes shortening the seasoning period for entry eligibility to six months from 12 months.

The consultation is open for public feedback until Thursday, May 28, 2026.

The news comes as U.S. regulators try to make IPO and capital raise rules easier for companies by cutting disclosure requirements or accelerating the registration process.

data.title: GoDaddy dips after Q1 results; analysts remain largely neutral
data.description:
Shares of GoDaddy (GDDY) fell about 2% on Friday after first quarter results, while analysts were largely neutral.

The firm kept its Equal Weight rating on the stock but raised the price target to $83 from $77.

"GDDY reported in-line 1Q EPS, while guiding 2Q bookings to a significant re-acceleration (at least +6% y/y vs. 1Q +3%) on lower promo activity. While bookings guide is +VE, ramping competitive intensity suggests further volatility could lie ahead," said analysts led by Alec Brondolo.

The analysts noted that GoDaddy guided second-quarter revenue/EBITDA 1%/3%, respectively, ahead of consensus at the high end, while not changing its full-year guidance. The analysts believe the lack of flow through to full-year guidance is a function of conservatism rather than an expectation of weaker second-half results.

"Mgmt to begin marketing Airo AI Builder in May, but will find cost savings to offset investments (AI efficiencies in other areas of the business), allowing for continued margin expansion. Expect customer count to continue to grow q/q in 2Q - 4Q, but only modestly," said Brondolo and his team.

data.title: Clorox sees more work ahead, lowers FY26 outlook
data.description:
Shares of Clorox (CLX) are under pressure on Friday after the company’s fiscal third-quarter results  and outlook for the year suggested continued headwinds for the consumer products company.

"Our third-quarter results were mixed, with continued momentum in some parts of our portfolio and slower-than-anticipated market share recovery in others," said Clorox CEO Linda Rendle. "Looking ahead, we recognize there is more work to do in what continues to be a challenging consumer and cost environment.”

While the company realized modest gains in health and wellness and household product sales, the gains were offset by a 9% decline in its Lifestyle segment, leaving net sales flat from a year ago, although slightly better than anticipated.

Clorox (CLX) earned an increased profit of $1.64 per share as aggressive cost-cutting in advertising and administrative expenses was partially offset by higher manufacturing and logistics costs.

Moreover, net cash generated by operations was cut by more than half to $282M, primarily due to a termination payment tied to the Glad joint venture.

For the rest of the year, Clorox (CLX) now expects net sales to be down 6% versus the prior outlook for sales to be at the lower end of the -6% to -10% range. Organic sales are now expected to be down 9% versus the prior range of down 5% to down 9%.

Gross margin is now expected to be down 250-300 basis points versus prior guidance of down 50-100 basis points.

Adjusted EPS outlook for FY26 was also lowered to include a 2-4 cent dilution from the GOJO acquisition, as well as higher interest rate expense associated with the debt financing for this deal. Adjusted earnings are now expected to be within a range of $5.45 and $5.65 per share from $5.95 and $6.30 per share previously, with a midpoint of $5.55 for the new range below the $5.69 consensus estimate.

Shares were down 9% at Friday's open, touching down at an 11-year low.

data.title: Apple's second quarter report puts Tech ETFs in the spotlight
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) shares surged nearly 4% on Friday after the company posted its best March quarter on record, delivering $111.2B in revenue, up 17% year-over-year and well ahead of the $109.46B consensus.

The blowout print is a direct tailwind for the group of tech-focused ETFs carrying significant Apple exposure.

Below is a list of ETFs with significant exposure to Apple (AAPL), focused on the technology sector. The list is arranged by their YTD performance and includes market capitalization details, recent price movement, and quant ratings.

The list is topped by Fidelity MSCI Information Technology Index ETF (FTEC), with a YTD performance of 9.63%. Vanguard Information Technology Index Fund ETF (VGT) and iShares US Technology ETF (IYW) follow closely behind, rounding out the top three performers. Manzil Russell Halal USA Broad Market ETF (MNZL) and Global X PureCap MSCI Information Technology ETF (GXPT) occupy the middle of the pack.

At the bottom of the list sits Roundhill AAPL WeeklyPay ETF (AAPW), which has significantly underperformed with a YTD loss of 10.01% and carries a Sell rating.

Here is the list:

  1. Fidelity MSCI Information Technology Index ETF (FTEC), YTD perf: 9.63%, Quant rating: Buy 4.32

  2. Vanguard Information Technology Index Fund ETF (VGT), YTD perf: 9.51%, Quant rating: Buy 4.34

  3. iShares US Technology ETF (IYW), YTD perf: 9.23%, Quant rating: Buy 4.36

  4. Manzil Russell Halal USA Broad Market ETF (MNZL), YTD perf: 7.20%, Quant rating: Not Covered

  5. Global X PureCap MSCI Information Technology ETF (GXPT), YTD perf: 6.21%, Quant rating: Not Covered

  6. VanEck Technology TruSector ETF (TRUT), YTD perf: 6.01%, Quant rating: Not Covered

  7. iShares S&P 500 Info Technology Sector UCITS ETF USD A (ISRCF), YTD perf: 5.69%, Quant rating: Buy 4.34

  8. Roundhill AAPL WeeklyPay ETF (AAPW), YTD perf: -10.01%, Quant rating: Sell 2.20

data.title: ISM Manufacturing PMI flatlines in April, missing consensus
data.description:
April U.S. ISM Manufacturing PMI: 52.7 vs. 53.0 consensus and 52.7 prior, according to data released by the Institute for Supply Management on Friday.

Manufacturing prices:  84.6 vs. 78.3 in March.

Employment: 46.4 vs. 48.7 prior.

New orders: 54.1 vs. 53.5 prior.

Supplier deliveries: 60.6  vs. 58.9 prior.

Developing… Check back for updates.

data.title: Salesforce goes all-in on AI as FY27 revenue model gets reset: Full details here
data.description:
• Salesforce (CRM) released an investor presentation on Friday stating that it will change its revenue reporting structure from FY27 to better reflect its AI-focused “Agentic Enterprise” strategy.
  • The new structure will mainly split revenue into two buckets: “Agentforce Apps” and “Data 360, Platform & Other."
  • The company will group products like Sales, Slack, MuleSoft, and Tableau under broader AI-focused categories while separately reporting major acquisition revenue like Informatica for one year after closing.
  • As per the presentation, recast FY26 data showed “Agentforce Apps” revenue grew 7% to about $26.7B, while “Data 360, Platform & Other” grew faster at 15% to nearly $12.7B.
  • Total subscription and support revenue rose 10% to around $39.4B in FY26.
  • It will also provide both old and new reporting formats during FY27 for easier comparison before fully switching to the new AI-focused structure in FY28.
  • Following the restructuring news, the shares jumped ~3.34% to ~$182.42.

data.title: Summit Therapeutics falls after update on Akeso-partnered lung cancer trial
data.description:
Summit Therapeutics (SMMT) lost ~22% on Friday after the company said that a late-stage trial for its lead asset, ivonescimab, developed with Chinese biotech Akeso (AKESF), will continue as planned following an uneventful interim analysis.

After an early analysis of its progression-free survival data, the Independent Data Monitoring Committee of its HARMONi-3 trial recommended that the study be continued as planned, the Florida-based biotech said with its Q1 2026 results on Thursday.

The Phase 3 global trial was designed to evaluate ivonescimab in combination with chemotherapy as a first-line option for metastatic non-small cell lung cancer. Its early PFS analysis was expected to support regulatory discussions ahead of the final PFS readout scheduled for H2 2026.

Wall Street analysts had mixed reactions to the update. “The interim PFS analysis was an important potential catalyst that investors were looking forward to and the optics of the new guidance pressured shares," Barclays analyst Etzer Darout wrote with an Equal Weight rating and an $18 target on SMMT.

“There is no change to the timing of the pre-planned final PFS analysis in H2:26," TD Cowen’s Tyler Van Buren added with a Buy rating, according to Bloomberg.

“Furthermore, we anticipate no change to this trial’s probability of success, especially given the minimal alpha spent on the interim analysis," Van Buren argued.

“Summit surprisingly missed interim PFS in major global trial HARMONi-3," Jefferies analyst Faisal Khurshid wrote of trimming his price target on Summit (SMMT) to $17 from $18 per share.
